body.conclusion: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Revised Cardano Summit 2026 Singapore" Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action Constitutional.
body.counterargumentDiscussion:
A reviewer might argue that the 80% kickoff milestone (6,240,000 ADA, available upon enactment with no delivery conditions) renders the cancellation refund scenario largely theoretical. Once CF claims Milestone 1 to secure the venue and pay vendor deposits, those funds are committed; Refund Scenario 1 applies only to the remaining balance. The Constitution requires specification of refund circumstances, not that a refund be available for the full withdrawal. The circumstances are specified; the front-loaded structure is an execution risk, not a constitutional deficiency.

A reviewer might further argue that the prior funding disclosure, providing Adastat links without stating ADA amounts inline, places the verification burden on the reader rather than satisfying the spirit of Article II.7.2. The constitutional text requires disclosure of "whether" prior receipt occurred; CF confirms it did. The quality of disclosure is a policy consideration rather than a constitutional one.

A reviewer might also note, as a policy observation, that Summit 2026 revenue is ring-fenced for 2027 event deposits rather than returned to the Treasury. The Constitution does not require revenue from treasury-funded events to return to the Treasury. This is a matter for DRep consideration.
body.precedentDiscussion: The SundaeSwap treasury.ak/vendor.ak custody structure and four-member Oversight Committee model were reviewed and accepted in a prior rationale covering the original Summit 2026 proposal under Articles II.7.5 and II.7.6. The constitutional findings on custody and administration carry forward to this revised version.
body.rationaleStatement:
This Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action requests 7,800,000 ADA ($1,950,000 USD at $0.25/ADA) to fund the Cardano Summit 2026 in Singapore on October 5 and 6, 2026, submitted by the Cardano Foundation. The gross budget is $2,263,000, offset by $313,000 in Summit 2025 revenue, yielding the $1,950,000 net ask. This is a revised version of a prior proposal, reduced by 22% ($550,000) following community feedback, narrowing from three stages to two. The Cardano Foundation is both proposer and sole vendor, administered through the SundaeSwap treasury.ak/vendor.ak escrow with an independent four-member Oversight Committee (DQuadrant, Intersect, NMKR, Sundae Labs).

The relevant provisions are Article I.1, Article II.6, Article II.7, and Appendix I TREASURY guardrails.

**Article I.1 (Guiding Tenets -- Tenet 8).** The Constitution states that "proposed governance actions shall be evaluated in accordance with these Tenets," including Tenet 8: "The Cardano Blockchain shall not unreasonably spend resources." CF's constitutional self-assessment contends that Tenet 8 applies only to protocol-level efficiency (compute, fees, operational costs) and that economic justification of spending is solely within DRep remit. The text of Tenet 8 does not support that limitation; "resources" is unqualified and treasury funds are resources of the Cardano Blockchain. That said, the standard is "unreasonably spend," which is a high bar. A two-day flagship event in a major global financial hub, timed to coincide with TOKEN2049, at a revised net budget of $1,950,000, does not meet that threshold. Tenet 8 is satisfied.

**Article II.6.1 (Metadata Format).** Metadata is IPFS-anchored at QmQeNSMNUDWy75tgjPLqqUXDN7YvkVH6GS8iHx6P8adCAy; Koios confirms meta_is_valid true, meaning the on-chain hash matches the document (62f5924317b9b984417db994f1a011a362fbfb36a01745e445ddd55033c75aea). Conforms to CIP-0108.

**Article II.6.2 (Metadata Structure).** Title, abstract, motivation, and rationale are present. The rationale includes a line-item budget comparing revised and prior figures, a KPI framework, a milestone schedule, and a constitutionality self-assessment. Sufficient.

**Article II.7.1 (Terms of the Withdrawal).** Purpose (Cardano Summit 2026, Singapore, October 5-6), delivery period, itemized costs, and refund circumstances are all stated. Three refund scenarios are specified: cancellation before vendor payments are due (balance returned); total expenses under the request (unspent ADA returned within six months); ADA price appreciation profit (returned within six months). Two milestones: Milestone 1 of 6,240,000 ADA available upon enactment; Milestone 2 of 1,560,000 ADA payable after event delivery and reporting. Satisfied.

**Article II.7.2 (Prior Funding Disclosure).** The Cardano Foundation discloses two prior 2025 treasury receipts by name and Adastat links: the "Unveiling the First Unified Global Events Marketing Strategy for Cardano" proposal and the "Cardano Summit 2025 and regional tech events" proposal. Prior receipt is confirmed. The amounts of ADA received under each governance action are not stated in the proposal text; the disclosure is by reference to external links. The Constitution's text ("shall disclose whether") is met. The absence of inline amounts is noted.

**Article II.7.3 (Net Change Limit Compliance).** The 350,000,000 ADA Net Change Limit (epochs 613-713) applies. 7,800,000 ADA is well within it.

**Article II.7.4 (Audits and Oversight).** The proposal allocates $100,000 (400,000 ADA) to a "Legal, Audit, Tax" line item. The three categories are not disaggregated, so the portion specifically reserved for periodic independent audits of this withdrawal cannot be verified from the proposal text. The audit mechanism described is the Cardano Foundation's existing annual Grant Thornton engagement, supplemented by a public treasury withdrawal dashboard (treasurywithdrawals.cardanofoundation.org). An organizational annual audit covers CF's operations broadly, including any treasury withdrawals. The oversight metrics requirement is satisfied by the public dashboard and milestone reporting. The audit allocation requirement is met, though the bundled line makes independent verification of the audit-specific amount unavailable.

**Article II.7.5 (Administrator).** The Cardano Foundation is designated as administrator and sole vendor. The Oversight Committee (DQuadrant, Intersect, NMKR, Sundae Labs) provides external checks: the Disburse operation requires 3 of 4 member signatures; any single member can pause a milestone. Satisfied.

**Article II.7.6 (Custody).** Funds held in SundaeSwap treasury.ak and disbursed via vendor.ak. Auto-abstain DRep delegation enforced at contract level. No SPO delegation. Unused funds sweep to the Cardano Treasury at expiration. Satisfied.

**Appendix I (Guardrails).** TREASURY-02a: 7,800,000 ADA does not exceed the Net Change Limit. TREASURY-03a: Withdrawal denominated in ADA. TREASURY-01a: not engaged.
body.summary: Ace Alliance finds the proposed "Revised Cardano Summit 2026 Singapore" Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action Constitutional. Rationales are archived at https://github.com/ace-alliance/ace-voting/
